sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: James Fishback’s Azoria ETFs collapsed months after launch amid legal, financial scrutiny
feed.summary: James Fishback's Azoria Capital dissolved its two main exchange-traded funds (ETFs) less than six months after their launch due to limited investor interest and ongoing legal challenges faced by Fishback, who is also a Florida gubernatorial candidate. The liquidation comes after a federal judge ordered Fishback to surrender Azoria stock to satisfy a judgment owed to his former employer, Greenlight Capital. Fishback's political campaign is also facing scrutiny over fundraising, personal finances, and controversial rhetoric.

feed.title: Texas sues Sanofi for allegedly 'bribing' providers to boost drug prescriptions
feed.summary: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has filed a lawsuit against Sanofi, accusing the French drugmaker of "bribing" healthcare providers to prescribe its medications over competitors. The lawsuit alleges that Sanofi's programs offering services to providers violated the Texas Health Care Program Fraud Prevention Act. Sanofi denies the allegations, stating its services comply with laws and support patients without influencing prescribing decisions.

feed.title: Copart (CPRT) Stock Falls After Q4 Earnings Report - Revenue and EPS Below Estimates - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Copart (CPRT) stock declined by 3.5% after its Q4 earnings report revealed revenue of $1.12 billion and earnings per share of $0.36, both falling below Wall Street expectations. The company also experienced a contracted operating margin and missed adjusted EBITDA estimates, leading to a negative investor reaction. Despite historically low volatility, the significant price movement indicates the market's concern over these financial results, with the stock now 4% down year-to-date and 43.2% below its 52-week high.

feed.title: Sprott (NYSE:SII) Trading 5.5% Higher - Still a Buy?
feed.summary: Sprott Inc. (NYSE:SII) shares rose 5.5% during intraday trading, reaching a high of $141.86, though trading volume was significantly below average. Despite a "Moderate Buy" consensus from analysts and a recent "Outperform" upgrade from RBC, the current market price is above the average target price of $132. The company also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.40 per share, representing an annualized yield of 1.1% and a payout ratio of 82.05%.

feed.title: Regal Rexnord Soars As JPMorgan Flags $200 Million Humanoid Robot Opportunity
feed.summary: Regal Rexnord Corp (NYSE: RRX) has seen its stock surge by 67% over the past three months, driven by increased investor interest in robotics. JPMorgan analyst Tomohiko Sano highlights the company's significant exposure to humanoid robots, with over $30 million in annual related business and a potential pipeline of $200 million across various robotics platforms. Regal Rexnord benefits from its role as a key supplier of precision motion systems, which are essential components for robotic function across multiple industries.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Lowers Visteon (NASDAQ:VC) Price Target to $125.00
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has lowered its price target for Visteon (NASDAQ:VC) to $125.00 from $148.00, while maintaining an "overweight" rating. Despite Visteon beating Q4 earnings and revenue estimates and raising its dividend, the stock was pressured by management's lower-than-expected revenue guidance for FY 2026 and concerns about memory costs and launch risks. The average analyst target for Visteon is now $122.15, with mixed sentiment among analysts.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Lowers Pool (NASDAQ:POOL) Price Target to $230.00
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has reduced its price target for Pool (NASDAQ:POOL) to $230.00 from $275.00, while maintaining an "equal weight" rating. This adjustment follows Pool's Q4 financial results, which missed both EPS and revenue consensus estimates, and the company's FY2026 EPS guidance fell below market expectations. Despite a mixed analyst outlook, institutional ownership remains high, with notable investment from Berkshire Hathaway.

feed.title: SMR Stock News: NuScale Power Corporation Sued for Securities Fraud after 12% Stock Drop -- Investors Notified to Contact BFA Law by April 20 Class Action Deadline
feed.summary: Bleichmar Fonti & Auld LLP has filed a class action lawsuit against NuScale Power Corporation and its executives for securities fraud, following a significant 12% stock drop. The lawsuit alleges that NuScale misrepresented ENTRA1 Energy LLC's experience in developing power plants, leading to a $495 million payment for services from a company that allegedly lacked substantial project experience. Investors in NuScale Class A common stock have until April 20, 2026, to seek appointment as lead plaintiff in the case.

feed.title: Celsior Technologies Joins the Jack Henry Fintech Integration Network
feed.summary: Celsior Technologies has joined the Jack Henry Fintech Integration Network (FIN), gaining access to technical resources for integrating its solutions with Jack Henry core platforms. This partnership aims to help financial institutions modernize digital workflows, improve operational efficiency, and accelerate technology initiatives while maintaining data integrity and security. By participating in FIN, Celsior enables banks to deploy solutions with reduced complexity and faster time to value.

feed.title: Raymond James Calls Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. (TTWO) Pullback Overdone Amid AI Concerns
feed.summary: Raymond James has upgraded Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. (TTWO) to Strong Buy, maintaining a $285 price target, despite recent stock pullback driven by AI concerns. The firm believes the market's reaction to Google's Project Genie and AI content creation tools is excessive, especially given TTWO's strong fiscal third-quarter performance. Additionally, a significant transfer of TTWO shares from Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund to its fully owned Savvy Games Group aims to strengthen Savvy's role in the global gaming industry, without materially changing overall Saudi ownership.

feed.title: City Council OKs CSX rail line purchase
feed.summary: The St. Petersburg City Council has approved the $7 million acquisition of a 0.86-mile section of a CSX rail corridor, which will be converted into a public walking and biking trail. The Trust for Public Land will first purchase the property from CSX, then transfer it to the City. Funding will come from the City, Ferg’s Sports Bar and Grill, and Ellison Development, with the City planning to seek state and federal grants for trail construction.

feed.title: Why Instacart (CART) Shares Are Plunging Today
feed.summary: Instacart (CART) shares dropped by 8.7% after reports surfaced that DoorDash and Kroger are expanding their grocery delivery partnership, directly challenging Instacart's position as Kroger is its third-largest customer. This development intensifies existing competitive pressures from major players like Amazon and Walmart. Despite previous strategic moves like a partnership with Vroom Delivery to expand its advertising business, concerns about market share erosion persist.

feed.title: Earnings recap: DoorDash, Walmart, Analog Devices, Rio Tinto, Carvana…
feed.summary: This article provides an earnings recap for several major companies including DoorDash, Walmart, Analog Devices, Rio Tinto, and Carvana. It details their financial performance for recent quarters, highlighting revenues, earnings per share, and stock reactions. Key updates include DoorDash missing analyst expectations, Walmart issuing a cautious outlook, and Analog Devices exceeding forecasts.

feed.title: Beyond the Box: How FedEx Is Winning as Tech Slumps
feed.summary: FedEx (FDX) is experiencing significant growth and stock price appreciation amid a slump in the tech sector, driven by strategic initiatives. Key factors include the upcoming spin-off of its high-margin Less-Than-Truckload (LTL) unit, FedEx Freight, and the Network 2.0 restructuring program aimed at reducing costs. The company is also aggressively expanding its market share in Europe through the acquisition of a stake in InPost S.A., demonstrating a strong focus on operational efficiency and shareholder value.

feed.title: RGNX SHAREHOLDER ALERT: Bronstein, Gewirtz and Grossman, LLC Announces that REGENXBIO Inc. Investors with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead Class Action Lawsuit!
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has announced a class action lawsuit against REGENXBIO Inc. (NASDAQ:RGNX) and its officers. The lawsuit alleges that REGENXBIO made false and misleading statements regarding its RGX-111 gene therapy for Hurler syndrome. Investors who purchased REGENXBIO securities between February 9, 2022, and January 27, 2026, have until April 14, 2026, to seek to be appointed as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Warns QUALCOMM Incorporated (QCOM) Upside Largely Priced In at Current Levels
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has resumed coverage of QUALCOMM Incorporated (QCOM) with an Underweight rating and a $132 price target, cautioning that the stock's upside is largely priced in and foreseeing difficulties for Android smartphones in late 2026 due to memory constraints. Separately, Qualcomm announced it would invest up to $150 million in Indian deep tech and AI firms through its Strategic AI Venture Fund. The company is highlighted as a semiconductor leader crucial for the metaverse, but analysts suggest other AI stocks may offer better upside with less risk.

feed.title: Rosenblatt Cuts The Walt Disney Company (DIS) PT Despite Q1 Earnings Beat
feed.summary: Rosenblatt analyst Barton Crockett reduced his price target on Disney (DIS) to $130 from $139, despite the company exceeding Q1 earnings expectations, citing a lack of passion in management's tone. Disney reaffirmed its double-digit EPS growth forecast for the full year, with a projected slowdown in Q2 and recovery in the second half. The company also recently issued a cease-and-desist letter to ByteDance concerning alleged unauthorized use of its intellectual property for AI training.

feed.title: Beyond the Box: How FedEx Is Winning as Tech Slumps
feed.summary: FedEx (FDX) is experiencing significant market gains and decoupling from the broader transportation index while the tech sector slumps. These gains are driven by strategic restructuring, including the spin-off of its FedEx Freight unit and aggressive cost-cutting through its Network 2.0 initiative. Additionally, FedEx is expanding its presence in Europe via a strategic acquisition of InPost S.A.'s parcel locker network, positioning it for growth in e-commerce and improving last-mile delivery economics.

feed.title: PPL Earnings Miss Estimates in Q4, Revenues Increase Y/Y
feed.summary: PPL Corporation reported Q4 2025 operating earnings per share of 41 cents, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 2.4%, though revenues increased 2.8% year-over-year to $2.27 billion. Despite the earnings miss, the company saw increased electricity sales and lower operating expenses in the quarter. PPL provided 2026 earnings guidance of $1.90-$1.98 per share and raised its planned infrastructure investments to $23 billion for 2026-2029.

feed.title: Rosenblatt Cuts The Walt Disney Company (DIS) PT Despite Q1 Earnings Beat
feed.summary: Rosenblatt analyst Barton Crockett reduced his price target for Disney (DIS) to $130 from $139, despite the company exceeding Q1 earnings expectations. The analyst cited concerns about management's tone, even though Disney reaffirmed its double-digit EPS growth forecast for the year, anticipating a recovery in the second half. Separately, Disney issued a cease-and-desist letter to ByteDance for alleged copyright infringement in training its AI video model, Seedance 2.0, which ByteDance subsequently addressed by limiting certain platform features.

feed.title: Invitation Homes stock hits 52-week low at $25.28
feed.summary: Invitation Homes Inc. (INVH) stock has fallen to a 52-week low of $25.28, representing a 17.24% decline over the past year. Despite recording positive Q4 2025 earnings which exceeded analyst expectations, the stock experienced a downturn. Analyst firms KeyBanc and Citizens have both revised down their price targets for Invitation Homes, citing market underperformance and oversupply pressures in the real estate sector.

feed.title: Beware the business school case study: the cautionary tale of Southwest Airlines
feed.summary: Business school case studies often present a biased view of success, focusing on companies approved cases and overlooking their eventual decline. The author argues that like Dell and Nokia, Southwest Airlines, once an exemplar, now faces challenges and a departure from its original values, exemplified by its recent Super Bowl ad mocking its historical differentiator. The article suggests that relying solely on past case studies without understanding the transient nature of competitive advantage can be misleading.

feed.title: Permianville Royalty: Is This 20%+ Yield Oil Trust a Value Trap?
feed.summary: Permianville Royalty Trust (PVL) offers a high yield that appears tempting, especially as oil prices stabilize. However, the trust faces significant risks including shrinking distributions, delisting to the OTC market which reduces liquidity, and the absence of institutional analyst coverage. Investors should be aware that historical yields may not be sustainable, and PVL is better suited as a high-risk, high-income satellite position rather than a stable core energy investment.

feed.title: Hospitality Mattress Market | Global Market Analysis Report - 2035
feed.summary: The global hospitality mattress market is projected to grow from USD 7.0 billion in 2025 to USD 12.7 billion by 2035, exhibiting a CAGR of 6.1%. This growth is fueled by increasing global tourism, demand for premium guest experiences, and focus on sleep quality in hospitality. Foam mattresses are expected to dominate by type, and queen-size mattresses by size, reflecting industry preferences for comfort, durability, and space optimization.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Shakes Claim It Fired Workers Who Backed Union
feed.summary: The National Labor Relations Board dismissed an unfair labor practice claim against Wells Fargo & Co. The claim was brought by nonbranch-based employees who alleged that the bank fired members of their proposed bargaining unit before a successful unionization vote in December 2024. Wells Fargo is noted as the first major US bank with unionized employees, with 30 branches having voted to unionize so far.

feed.title: Copart Inc (NASDAQ:CPRT) Lags Estimates
feed.summary: Copart Inc (NASDAQ:CPRT) reported earnings and revenue below Wall Street estimates for the quarter ending January 31, 2026, leading to a 4.46% stock drop. The company's net income fell 9.5% and operating income declined 8.8%, despite recording record U.S. insurance average selling prices. This underperformance was attributed to softer claims activity and lower global insurance unit volumes.

feed.title: Newmont Corporation (NYSE:NEM) Forecast Lower Production
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation (NYSE:NEM) saw its stock fall 4.04% despite beating Wall Street's fourth-quarter profit estimates, driven by record gold prices that offset lower production. The company announced a $1.4 billion investment in newly acquired assets and forecast lower gold production for 2026. Newmont also reported decreases in gold, copper, and silver reserves and resources compared to the previous year.

feed.title: Brianne Gardner's Top Picks: ATS Corp, NRG Energy & Netflix
feed.summary: Brianne Gardner, a senior wealth manager at Velocity Investment Partners, Raymond James, has revealed his top stock picks. The article highlights his selections: ATS Corp, NRG Energy, and Netflix, offering insights for investors.

feed.title: Walmart Inc (NASDAQ:WMT) Issues Cautious Outlook
feed.summary: Walmart Inc (NASDAQ:WMT) saw its stock fall after issuing a cautious annual outlook for fiscal year 2026, which disappointed Wall Street despite solid fourth-quarter results. The company reported strong Q4 FY25 performance with adjusted EPS of 74 cents and revenue of $190.7 billion, both beating estimates. However, the projected net sales growth of 3.5%-4.5% and adjusted earnings between US$2.75 and US$2.85 per share for FY26 were below analyst expectations, raising concerns about sustaining growth under the new CEO.

feed.title: Stocks making big moves this week: Xerox, Vulcan Materials, Mercury General, Wendy's, and Wingstop
feed.summary: This article highlights several stocks that experienced significant movement this week. Xerox rose after a joint venture and new sales structure. Conversely, Vulcan Materials and Mercury General both fell due to disappointing earnings and outlooks. Wendy's surged on news of activist investor interest and a potential takeover, while Wingstop saw its stock increase after strong profit beats in its quarterly earnings.

feed.title: Merck vs. Bristol Myers: Which Pharma Stock Is a Better Pick in 2026?
feed.summary: This article compares Merck (MRK) and Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) as investment options for 2026, analyzing their fundamentals, growth outlooks, risks, and valuations. While Merck boasts strong oncology sales driven by Keytruda and a robust pipeline, it faces headwinds from patent expirations and declining sales of other drugs. Bristol Myers Squibb also has a strong oncology portfolio and is working to mitigate generic competition for legacy drugs through acquisitions and cost savings. Considering valuation, dividend yield, and near-term outlook, Bristol Myers Squibb is presented as the more compelling investment choice.

feed.title: Lam Research Named Top Semiconductor Pick As AI-Driven Manufacturing Supercycle Shifts Into High Gear
feed.summary: Benzinga editor Anusuya Lahiri reports that Vivek Arya of BofA Securities has named Lam Research (NASDAQ: LRCX) as his top semiconductor pick. This is due to a strong, multi-year wafer fab equipment (WFE) upcycle driven by AI and advanced chip demand. Lam Research is poised to benefit from this surge, with a projected industry-wide spending of $135 billion in WFE by 2026 and potential spillover into 2027.

feed.title: Sbrocco, Westinghouse Air Brake EVP, sells $246k in WAB stock
feed.summary: Gregory Sbrocco, EVP of Operations at Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies CORP (NASDAQ:WAB), sold 952 shares of WAB stock for over $246,000. This transaction occurred on February 19, 2026, with the stock trading near its 52-week high after significant gains. Despite the sale, Sbrocco still directly owns 21,159 shares, and the company recently reported strong Q4 2025 financial results, leading to a raised price target from Stephens.

feed.title: PWR: Baird Raises Price Target on Quanta Services to $606 | PWR Stock News
feed.summary: Baird has raised its price target on Quanta Services (PWR) to $606.00 from $490.00, while maintaining an "Outperform" rating. This decision reflects a positive sentiment, aligning with other analysts who have also recently increased their price targets for the company. Despite the positive analyst outlook, GuruFocus estimates suggest a potential downside for PWR based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Why Procore Technologies (PCOR) Stock Is Up Today
feed.summary: Procore Technologies (PCOR) shares jumped 8.4% after the company announced Ajei Gopal as its new CEO, succeeding founder Tooey Courtemanche. This leadership transition was seen positively by analysts, who noted Gopal's experience in vertical software and scaling public companies. The company also reaffirmed its Q3 and full fiscal year 2025 guidance, signaling financial stability during the change.

feed.title: Nucor promotes Sullivan to CFO
feed.summary: Nucor Corp. has promoted Jack Sullivan to Chief Financial Officer and Executive Vice President, effective March 1. Sullivan joined the Charlotte-based steel manufacturer in 2022 and previously worked at Duke Energy and Bank of America. He succeeds Steve Laxton, who became President and COO on January 1, and will also continue in his role as treasurer.

feed.title: D-Wave Quantum (NYSE: QBTS) Joins Southeastern Quantum Collaborative as Inaugural Member
feed.summary: D-Wave Quantum Inc. (NYSE: QBTS) has become an inaugural member of the Southeastern Quantum Collaborative (SQC), an initiative to advance quantum information science and technology in the Southeast and develop a quantum-ready workforce. D-Wave joins partners like The University of Alabama in Huntsville, Davidson Technologies, IBM, and Alabama A&M University. The company emphasizes its role in supporting quantum workforce development, particularly as Davidson Technologies already hosts a D-Wave Advantage2™ system.

feed.title: Blue Owl's Redemption Shift Shakes Private Credit Industry
feed.summary: Blue Owl Capital Inc. has disrupted the private credit market by restricting investor withdrawals from one of its retail-focused funds, leading to a sell-off in its shares and those of other alternative asset managers like Apollo, Blackstone, KKR, and Ares. This move, prompted by rising redemption requests and liquidity pressures, shifts focus from credit performance to structural liquidity risk and suggests a reassessment of profit expectations in the rapidly expanding private credit sector. The market is now prioritizing liquidity management and fund structure as much as yield and credit quality.

feed.title: GLPI “open for business” in New York City
feed.summary: Gaming & Leisure Properties Inc. (GLPI) is actively exploring investment opportunities in New York City's burgeoning casino market, particularly interested in Bally's Bronx and other potential projects, but emphasizes that its loans would require partial ownership or purchase options. Despite Bally's current negative cash flow and stock trading at a discount, GLPI maintains a long-term view, citing ongoing development and confidence in properties like Bally's Chicago and the planned Las Vegas resort. The company also discussed other market dynamics, including Virginia's potential igaming legislation and the Canadian casino market, while asserting that their investment decisions are not solely based on interest rates or a tenant's balance sheet percentage.

feed.title: MGRX (Mangoceuticals, Inc., NASDAQ) up 50.75% intraday 20 Feb 2026: TRT sales drive heavy volume
feed.summary: Mangoceuticals, Inc. (MGRX) experienced a significant 50.75% intraday surge on February 20, 2026, driven by strong initial sales results from its $99 injectable TRT program. The company reported 336% month-over-month sales growth and a 54% reduction in customer acquisition cost, leading to a massive volume spike of nearly 300 million shares. While strong TRT sales propelled the stock, Meyka AI's model projects a 12-month fair value of USD 0.26, indicating potential downside due to ongoing net losses and liquidity risks.

feed.title: Helix Energy Solns Gr Earnings Preview
feed.summary: Helix Energy Solutions Group (NYSE: HLX) is set to release its quarterly earnings on February 23, 2026. Analysts project an earnings per share (EPS) of $0.02, and investors will be looking for the company to surpass estimates and provide positive guidance for the next quarter. The company's stock has shown positive performance, up 13.44% over the last 52 weeks, despite missing EPS estimates by $0.01 in the previous quarter.

feed.title: Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) Stock Price Expected to Rise, Wells Fargo & Company Analyst Says
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has raised its price target for Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) to $248 from $207, maintaining an "overweight" rating and suggesting a 7.7% upside. This follows Targa's strong Q4 EPS beat, record 2025 results, a dividend increase, and a bullish 2026 outlook. While the stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts, some institutional investors have recently adjusted their stakes in the company.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) Given New $47.00 Price Target at Wells Fargo & Company
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has raised its price target for Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) from $40.00 to $47.00, though it maintains an "underweight" rating, suggesting an 8.05% potential downside. Despite beating Q4 EPS expectations, Occidental Petroleum missed on revenue but announced an increased quarterly dividend and significant debt reduction. The consensus among analysts remains a "Hold" with an average target price of $48.52, indicating mixed views on the stock's future performance.

feed.title: A Look Ahead: SI-BONE's Earnings Forecast
feed.summary: SI-BONE (NASDAQ: SIBN) is scheduled to release its quarterly earnings report on Monday, February 23, 2026. Analysts project an EPS of $-0.13 for the company. Historically, positive earnings have led to a stock price increase, though shares are currently down 10.79% over the past year.

feed.title: Benchmark Cuts Hillman Solutions (NASDAQ:HLMN) Price Target to $14.00
feed.summary: Benchmark has lowered its price target for Hillman Solutions (NASDAQ:HLMN) to $14.00 from $15.00 while maintaining a "buy" rating, reflecting a potential 54% upside. This adjustment follows Hillman's recent quarterly earnings report, which met EPS estimates but slightly missed revenue expectations. Despite the target cut, institutional ownership remains very high, with several major funds increasing their stakes in recent quarters.

feed.title: What Does the Market Think About Immunic Inc?
feed.summary: Immunic Inc's (IMUX) short interest has decreased by 7.19% since its last report, now standing at 6.57 million shares sold short, which is 5.55% of its float. This decline suggests a more bullish sentiment among investors compared to the previous report. The company's short interest is also lower than its peer group average of 10.21%.

feed.title: Deep Dive Into Integer Holdings Stock: Analyst Perspectives (6 Ratings)
feed.summary: Integer Holdings (NYSE: ITGR) has been evaluated by 6 analysts, showing a mix of bullish and indifferent sentiments. The average 12-month price target is $88.67, an 8.13% increase from the previous average. The company demonstrates strong financial health with positive revenue growth of 8.41%, an excellent net margin of 8.48%, and efficient asset and equity utilization.

feed.title: Genco Shipping & Trading stock hits 52-week high at 23.94 USD
feed.summary: Genco Shipping & Trading Ltd's stock has reached a new 52-week high of $23.94 USD, reflecting a 67.77% increase over the past year and strong investor confidence. This surge occurs amidst a dispute where Genco rejected Diana Shipping Inc.'s unsolicited acquisition proposal of $20.60 per share, which Genco deemed undervalues the company. Genco's board is currently reviewing the proposal with advisors.

feed.title: AT&T Options Trading: A Deep Dive into Market Sentiment
feed.summary: Large investors have shown a mixed sentiment towards AT&T (NYSE: T) through recent options trades, with a split between bullish and bearish positions. Benzinga’s scanner identified 12 uncommon options trades, with a significant amount spent on both puts and calls targeting a price range of $22.0 to $30.0 over the next three months. The article also notes AT&T's current market standing, its primary business segments, and recent analyst ratings.

feed.title: Nutrien Announces Attendance at the BMO and BofA Investor Conferences
feed.summary: Nutrien Ltd. announced that its President and CEO, Ken Seitz, will speak at the BMO 35th Global Metals, Mining and Critical Minerals Conference on February 24, 2026. Mr. Seitz and CFO Mark Thompson will also present at the 2026 BofA Global Agriculture and Materials Conference on February 25, 2026. Video casts of both fireside chats will be available on Nutrien's investor relations website.
